task_completion.description: bcWebCam is launched and positioned over the barcode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application window is visible on screen and its capture area is placed over the barcode displayed in the web browser.

task_completion.description: The barcode is successfully scanned and decoded by bcWebCam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam interface displays the decoded 12-digit UPC number (049000000443).

task_completion.description: The local CSV database is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application or text editor is open displaying the contents of 'C:\workspace\openfoodfacts_subset.csv'.

task_completion.description: The row matching the scanned UPC is located in the CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The row containing the UPC '049000000443', 'Coca-Cola Original Taste', and '42' is visible in the CSV viewer, potentially highlighted or selected by the agent.

task_completion.description: The daily diary text file is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) is open displaying the file 'C:\workspace\daily_diary.txt'.

task_completion.description: The nutritional information is appended to the diary in the correct format and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the exact line '049000000443 - Coca-Cola Original Taste - 42 kcal' added to the file. There should be evidence the file was saved (e.g., clicking File -> Save, or the absence of an unsaved indicator like an asterisk in the window title).

integrity.description: The agent used the bcWebCam GUI to scan the barcode rather than bypassing the tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the bcWebCam UI actively capturing the screen and decoding the barcode, not just a terminal script or hallucinated output.

integrity.description: The agent opened and read the CSV file to find the product details.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'openfoodfacts_subset.csv' file is visibly opened in an application during the trajectory, proving the agent didn't just use prior knowledge to guess the Coca-Cola details.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI text editor to modify the diary file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A GUI text editor (like Notepad) is used to append the text to 'daily_diary.txt', rather than using hidden background scripts or terminal echo commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected UPC decoded from the barcode is '049000000443'. The corresponding product name in the CSV is 'Coca-Cola Original Taste' and the energy value is '42'. The exact line that must be appended to C:\workspace\daily_diary.txt is: '049000000443 - Coca-Cola Original Taste - 42 kcal'.
